articles.title: Houses, SUVs & more: How lifestyles of Ram temple theft accused changed dramatically
articles.description: An investigation into Ram Temple donations in Ayodhya has revealed a stark financial uplift among accused individuals, with some building homes and acquiring SUVs despite modest known incomes. Evidence suggests significant daily siphoning of funds, estimated at Rs 6 to Rs 8 lakh, particularly during the Maha Kumbh period. Concerns were raised about the handling of precious metal donations and the lack of proper oversight.
articles.full_text: When not covering crime, he reads fiction, roams around the dark alleys of the city in the dead of night. An MA (English Litt) from University of Lucknow, Pathikrit loves to speak French, well at least a smattering of it. He did his graduation in French language. A recipient of the Road Safety Fellowship of the United Nations, Pathikrit drives cautiously and advises others to do that too. Read More

articles.title: 'Paraguay's players were absolute disgrace': South American side slammed after WC exit
articles.description: France reached the quarter-finals with a tense 1-0 win over France national football team, but the match was overshadowed by the behaviour of Paraguay national football team. Joe Hart called Paraguay's players "an absolute disgrace", while Micah Richards described their conduct as "embarrassing". Kylian Mbappe scored the winning penalty to set up a quarter-final clash with Morocco.

articles.title: Why did Kai Cenat disappear from streaming? Everything ahead of his July 6 return stream
articles.description: Kai Cenat will return to streaming on July 6 at 8 PM EST after spending months away from live content. During the break, he focused on his fashion brand Vivet, personal growth and creative projects. His comeback stream is expected to reveal key Streamer University 2026 details and will likely be his first simultaneous broadcast on Twitch and YouTube.

articles.title: Candace Owens accuses Andrew Kolvet of ‘lying’ over Charlie Kirk claim in X dispute
articles.description: Candace Owens and Andrew Kolvet are publicly disputing what was said during a past private conversation involving Charlie Kirk. Owens says Kolvet told her about an offer to help take Turning Point USA “to the next level,” while Kolvet says that never happened and has demanded proof. No independent evidence has been publicly released by either side so far.

articles.title: Is Nocturnal Kent dead? Looksmaxxing streamer sparks concern after alleged brain aneurysm rumors
articles.description: Rumors about looksmaxxing streamer Nocturnal Kent's alleged death spread rapidly across social media after an image claiming to show him in an ICU bed surfaced online. Posts also alleged he suffered a brain aneurysm. However, no official statement from his family, representatives, or authorities has confirmed the reports. Until verified information is released, the claims remain unconfirmed, and fans have been urged to avoid sharing speculation as fact.

articles.title: Watch: New York's Brooklyn Bridge catches fire during July 4 fireworks display
articles.description: During Macy's Fourth of July fireworks extravaganza, the stunning Brooklyn Bridge briefly caught fire, sending flames and dark smoke billowing up as fireworks lit up the night sky above. Eyewitnesses reported that the fire began modestly but intensified quickly, with additional explosions reportedly triggered by the blaze. The dramatic scene unfolded over approximately one minute, leaving uncertainty about any potential damage to the historic structure.

articles.title: Ex-CFO of RCap, Amit Bapna, held in connection with ADAG probe
articles.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has apprehended Amit Bapna, former CFO of Reliance Capital Ltd, as part of its probe into Reliance ADA Group matters. Authorities allege Bapna, a key financial decision-maker, approved loans to intermediary firms despite knowing it violated RBI guidelines. Funds were reportedly diverted to various Reliance group entities, causing losses to banks and gains for the accused.

articles.title: India-UK trade pact: Centre notifies rules for determination of origin of goods
articles.description: India's Finance Ministry has finalized rules for goods eligible for benefits under the India-UK trade pact, set to begin July 15. This Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will grant duty-free access to 99% of Indian exports, boosting sectors like textiles and engineering. Exporters must now provide a certificate of origin to claim these tariff advantages, ensuring the pact's integrity and preventing misuse.

articles.title: Chip by chip: India now world's second largest phone maker; PM Modi lays out 'Make in India' electronics roadmap
articles.description: India has ascended to become the world's second-largest mobile phone manufacturer and exporter, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced, underscoring the nation's commitment to building a comprehensive electronics and semiconductor ecosystem. This strategic push, encompassing chip design, fabrication, and packaging, is a pivotal step towards a 'Viksit Bharat' and represents the next phase of the 'Make in India' initiative, with the country's youth poised to lead future technological advancements.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: In Kerala HC, NMC reiterates MBBS fees can be charged only for 4.5-year academic duration
articles.description: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has told the Kerala High Court that MBBS fees can be charged only for the prescribed four-and-a-half years of academic study, arguing that colleges cannot levy tuition beyond the notified course duration. The submission came in response to a petition by the Kerala Private Medical College Management Association. The Commission relied on the NMC Act, CBME Guidelines and previous court rulings to support its stand on MBBS fee collection.
articles.full_text: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has told the Kerala High Court that MBBS fees can be charged only for the prescribed four-and-a-half years of academic study, arguing that colleges cannot levy tuition beyond the notified course duration. The Commission relied on the NMC Act, CBME Guidelines and previous court rulings to support its stand on MBBS fee collection.

articles.title: Indian Navy Agniveer Apprentice recruitment 2026 application closes today for 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches; apply here
articles.description: The online application process for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 closes today, July 5, 2026, for the 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches. Eligible unmarried Indian male candidates with the required Class 10 qualification and AICTE-recognised engineering diploma can apply online. The recruitment process includes INET 2/2026, the Physical Fitness Test, a written examination and medical examination. The notification also specifies batch-wise age criteria, application fee and the complete recruitment schedule.

articles.title: RRB CEN 08/2025 exam city slip released for 311 Ministerial and Isolated Category posts; download here
articles.description: RRB CEN 08/2025 exam city slip: The Railway Recruitment Board has released the exam city intimation slip for the Ministerial and Isolated Category recruitment under CEN 08/2025 for 311 vacancies. Registered candidates can check their allotted examination city through the official portal ahead of the written examination scheduled for July 14 and 15, 2026. The recruitment covers seven posts with varying educational qualifications. The selection process includes CBT-I, CBT-II, a skill test where applicable, document verification and a medical examination.

articles.title: NIELIT O Level July 2026 admit card out at student.nielit.gov.in; candidates can download theory and practical hall tickets here
articles.description: The NIELIT O Level admit card for the July 2026 examination cycle has been released on the official student portal. Candidates can download their theory and practical hall tickets using their application or registration details and date of birth. The theory examination will begin on July 11, 2026, followed by practical examinations from August 08, 2026. The admit card includes examination shift, batch allocation and test centre details, while candidates must also follow the prescribed exam-day guidelines.

articles.title: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt Wedding Live Updates
Janhvi Kapoor and Khushi Kapoor plan special mehendi celebration for Anshula Kapoor
articles.description: Janhvi and Khushi Kapoor threw a wonderful surprise mehendi party for their sister Anshula Kapoor, gearing up for her wedding to Rohan Thakkar on July 6. Anshula, overjoyed, shared lovely snapshots showcasing the mix of Punjabi and Gujarati customs, reflecting her heartfelt gratitude to her sisters. The celebration brought together family and friends, with notable guests like Sonam Kapoor and Orry, in an atmosphere of love and excitement.

articles.title: ‘GDN’ trailer: Madhavan transforms into G.D. Naidu
articles.description: The launch of the official trailer for 'GDN', a thrilling biographical period drama with R. Madhavan portraying the esteemed inventor G.D. Naidu, is generating excitement. In a gripping nearly three-minute preview, the trailer delves into Naidu's motivating story, emphasizing his resilience and determination within the pre-independence landscape. Directed by Krishnakumar Ramakumar, the film includes notable performances by Sathyaraj and Priyamani, with Madhavan also taking a role as a co-screenwriter.

articles.title: Meghan Markle will not join Prince Harry for UK visit
articles.description: In a notable return, Prince Harry will travel to the UK next week for Invictus Games events, but he will do so without Meghan Markle or their young children, Archie and Lilibet. The decision reflects ongoing security issues and the end of royal protection outside royal residences. Although King Charles extended an invitation for accommodation, its security aspect is somewhat restricted.
